source: manual production pipeline run, 10-feed batch (my-manual-run-10 + follow-up backfill)
captured_by: operator (manual observation from in-flight + completed run logs)

workload_shape.provider_concurrency: sequential per-stage, multi-feed parallel via outer driver

gemini_503_retry_rate_pct.basis: operator observation across ~3h of batched calls; not extracted from per-call telemetry export

wall_clock_overhead_pct.basis: run completed cleanly via #697 circuit breaker; overhead vs throttle-free baseline estimated

headline_observations.completion: all episodes succeeded; circuit breaker absorbed bursts

per_call_telemetry.reason: operator did not export provider_metrics shard for this run; ProviderCallMetrics.record_retry() data is in-process only and was not persisted to a dedicated reliability file

what_this_evidence_supports: Reliability is not a tail risk — at sustained-load operating points, gemini-2.5-flash-lite shows a measurable 503 floor (~15-20%) that small-batch eval does not surface.
The #697 circuit breaker is load-bearing: without it, retries would cascade.
Effective throughput at the operating point is materially below nameplate single-call throughput.
what_this_evidence_does_not_support: Per-stage 503 attribution (summary vs gi vs kg vs speaker) — not measured.
Cross-model comparison — only one model in flight.
